summary:
The prognosis of occupational allergic contact dermatitis depends significantly on whether the allergen is consistently exposed; avoiding it leads to improved outcomes by reducing immunological reactivity. Persistent or omnipresent allergens increase risk of recurrent symptoms and long-term disease due to continuous immune activation.  

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant factors related directly to allergen presence and its impact on patient course.
